commands.review_summary:
The PR threads command-session metadata mutation notifications through auto-reply command/directive persistence into Gateway `sessions.changed` broadcasts, with related Gateway, UI, TUI, and slash-command doc coverage.

PR surface: Source +308, Tests +703, Docs +2. Total +1013 across 29 files.

Reproducibility: yes. source inspection gives a high-confidence reproduction path: command-only `/goal` mutations update the session store through command helpers on current main, while the existing Gateway `sessions.changed` emitter is only reached by session RPC/lifecycle paths.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] The remaining action is maintainer session-state review plus contributor or maintainer real-behavior proof, not a narrow automated code repair.

target.title: Emit sessions.changed for in-chat command metadata
target.body:
Fixes #88598

## User-facing bug

Control UI clients subscribed to session updates could miss metadata changes made by in-chat commands. A command-only turn such as `/goal complete`, `/goal pause`, or `/goal clear` can update the session store without starting a model run, but the Gateway did not emit a `sessions.changed` event for that mutation. The visible session row could keep stale goal/status metadata until a manual reload or another unrelated session event.

## Exact repro

Source-level reproduction:

1. Connect a Control UI/WebChat client that subscribes to `sessions.changed`.
2. Send an in-chat `/goal` command that mutates the persisted session goal, for example `/goal complete shipped`.
3. The command handler writes the updated goal to the session store and returns command output.
4. Before this patch, no command-metadata `sessions.changed` event was broadcast for the store write, so subscribed clients did not get a scoped refresh for that session.

## Fix summary

- Add a typed `onSessionMetadataChanged` reply option for command handlers.
- Notify after successful command-session store writes through the shared `persistSessionEntry` helper.
- Notify `/goal` start, pause, resume, complete, blocked, clear, and status paths when they actually change the persisted goal metadata.
- Share the Gateway `sessions.changed` emitter between session RPC handlers and `chat.send`, so command-only chat turns can broadcast a scoped session row update.
- Delay command-metadata broadcasts until after the current command turn leaves the active-run map, while still preserving `hasActiveRun: true` if another run for the same session is active.
- Fall back to the shared session lifecycle event bus when command metadata changes happen outside `chat.send`, so raw channel dispatchers that use the same command-session store helper also refresh Gateway subscribers.
- Preserve selected-agent global alias handling so canonical `global` updates still refresh a selected `agent:<id>:global` Control UI/WebChat session.
- Include live session setting metadata (`thinkingLevels`, `thinkingOptions`, `thinkingDefault`) on command-metadata `sessions.changed` events, and reuse the TUI-loaded model catalog when rebuilding embedded session defaults.
- Document that command-only session metadata changes refresh subscribed clients.

## Targeted tests

- Added `/goal` command unit coverage for metadata-change notifications on mutating goal actions and no-op clear paths.
- Added `/goal` command unit coverage for the shared lifecycle fallback when no direct Gateway subscriber callback is installed.
- Added Gateway sessions coverage for command-metadata `sessions.changed` payloads and active-run exclusion.
- Added Gateway chat coverage proving command-only `/goal` turns broadcast scoped session updates and do not report their own command turn as active.
- Added Gateway lifecycle coverage proving command metadata lifecycle events are broadcast to session subscribers.
- Added UI controller coverage for canonical `global` deltas against selected agent-global aliases.
- Added embedded TUI coverage proving history model catalogs are reused for session metadata/defaults.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: In-chat command metadata writes now emit scoped `sessions.changed` events, so subscribed Control UI/WebChat clients can refresh visible session metadata after command-only turns.

Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw checkout on branch `fix/sessions-changed-in-chat-commands`, commit `934d2d9968`, rebased onto `origin/main` at `f6653b9b35`. The Gateway proof exercises the `sessions.changed` command-metadata path used by subscribed Control UI/WebChat clients.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:
- `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD`
- `./node_modules/.bin/oxlint src/gateway/server-methods/chat.ts src/tui/embedded-backend.test.ts`
- `CI=1 NODE_OPTIONS=--max-old-space-size=4096 node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.core.test.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core-test.tsbuildinfo`
- `CI=1 NODE_OPTIONS=--max-old-space-size=4096 OPENCLAW_HEAVY_CHECK_LOCK_SCOPE=worktree OPENCLAW_VITEST_NO_OUTPUT_TIMEOUT_MS=120000 OPENCLAW_VITEST_NO_OUTPUT_HEARTBEAT_MS=10000 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/gateway/server.sessions.list-changed.test.ts -t "sessions.changed command metadata"`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/tui/embedded-backend.test.ts src/gateway/server.sessions.list-changed.test.ts src/gateway/server.chat.gateway-server-chat.test.ts`
- `OPENCLAW_HEAVY_CHECK_LOCK_SCOPE=worktree node scripts/run-vitest.mjs ui/src/ui/controllers/chat.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/commands-session-store.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/commands-goal.test.ts src/tui/embedded-backend.test.ts`
- `codex review --base origin/main`

Evidence after fix:
- Focused Gateway command-metadata proof passed with local-network permission after the `f6653b9b35` rebase: `Test Files 1 passed (1)`, `Tests 4 passed | 14 skipped (18)`, duration `4.92s`. It verifies command metadata excludes hidden internal active runs, scopes untagged literal `global` runs to the default agent, preserves active selected global runs, and includes live session setting metadata.
- CI hygiene follow-up passed the exact touched surfaces: direct `oxlint` accepted the touched files, and `run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.core.test.json` exited successfully.
- Targeted TUI/Gateway regression run passed with elevated local-network permission: `passed 2 Vitest shards in 13.14s`, including `src/tui/embedded-backend.test.ts`, `src/gateway/server.sessions.list-changed.test.ts`, and `src/gateway/server.chat.gateway-server-chat.test.ts`.
- UI/controller plus command-store/goal regression run passed: `passed 3 Vitest shards in 8.42s`, covering `ui/src/ui/controllers/chat.test.ts`, `/goal` command tests, command-session store tests, and embedded TUI tests.
- Copied Gateway event output from the local WebSocket proof:

```json
{
  "type": "sessions.changed",
  "reason": "command-metadata",
  "sessionKey": "main",
  "hasActiveRun": false,
  "metadata": {
    "thinkingDefault": "medium",
    "thinkingLevels": ["low", "medium", "high"]
  }
}
```

- `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD` passed.
- `codex review --base origin/main` reported no discrete introduced bug in the changed command metadata, gateway session-change, or UI chat-event paths. Its own targeted Vitest wrapper run was blocked by local `.git/openclaw-local-checks` permission, while the focused Gateway proof above passed with elevated local-network permission.

Observed result after fix: Command-only `/goal` metadata mutations produce scoped `sessions.changed` broadcasts with fresh session row metadata. `chat.send` keeps the command-only active-run exclusion, selected agent-global aliases receive canonical global deltas, and non-`chat.send` command paths get the shared lifecycle fallback instead of leaving subscribers stale.

What was not tested: Full gateway/core suites and full `pnpm check` were not run. The older GitHub run for this PR showed `checks-node-agentic-control-plane-startup-health-runtime` timing out with no output; I could not rerun that workflow because GitHub reports failed-run reruns require repository admin rights, so I refreshed the branch onto the latest `origin/main` and reran the targeted local proof instead. `node scripts/run-oxlint-shards.mjs --threads=8` was attempted earlier but this local checkout lacks the `tsgolint` executable required by the shard wrapper, so I used direct `oxlint` on the touched files instead. I did not run a live browser Control UI session against a real long-running `/goal` command. The Gateway tests require local `127.0.0.1` listening; the default sandbox returned `listen EPERM`/home-state permission errors, so I reran the focused Gateway proof with elevated local-network permission. I did not implement or test `/name` because this checkout does not contain a `/name` handler.

## Not tested / known gaps

- This patch focuses on command metadata changes that happen through `chat.send` reply options and the shared command-session store helper.
- Channel command flows that bypass the shared command-session store helper are unchanged.
- Full gateway/core suites and full `pnpm check` were not run in this remediation pass.

## AI-assisted disclosure

This patch was prepared with AI assistance. I used source-level reproduction, targeted regression tests, and `codex review --base origin/main` to validate the behavior above.
